text:
**\
00:03**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Hey, how\'s it going? My.

**\
00:06**\
Mai Flynn\
Hey, how are you?

**\
00:08**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I am good. Give me just one second to sort out the speaker situation.

**\
00:16**\
Mai Flynn\
Where are you calling in from?

**\
00:18**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I\'m calling in from Brooklyn. How about yourself?

**\
00:21**\
Mai Flynn\
Calling in from Philly.

**\
00:23**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Nice. Okay. Right next door.

**\
00:26**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah, I visit pretty often. I have a good friend that lives in Bed Stuy.

**\
00:32**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Nice. Okay. Yeah, we\'re right next door. We\'re in downtown Brooklyn,
sort of Fort Greenish area. Is Lensa primarily a U.S. company or Europe
based?

**\
00:46**\
Mai Flynn\
So we are an American company. We only buy and sell US traffic. But we
do have a holy owned subsidiary in Hungary. So all of our current end
business happens in the US and then all of the development tech stuff
happens in Budapest.

**\
01:06**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Gotcha. Gotcha. Okay, that makes sense. I think I may have conflated
some details in my call with Peter, but you know, such a pleasure to
meet you. I really appreciate your time and patience while I was getting
my shift together. Let\'s just say for our part, careerspan is
effectively trying to build out a talent network. The way we do that is
we go to really high signal communities of professionals. So think
McKinsey alumni Zooglers, Ivy League Alumni Association. We\'re hoping
to expand out from there, but essentially go to these high signal
communities, offer our consumer product for free as a way to embed
within these communities and actually get a extremely rich data source
into who these folks are. Much more detailed than the resume that allows
us to match. Even here. Yeah, someone might be in here. I just need to
jump out.

**\
02:11**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Sorry, I just sat down. Feel free to jump in. Give me one second. No,
close. Okay.

**\
02:26**\
Mai Flynn\
The joys of shared workspaces.

**\
02:29**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I know, I know. Normally I\'m fine because I. Well, future fit did
invite us and. Okay, never mind. So I think I found a. I think I found a
good place because. Just going to snag the office of the folks that
invited us here. We go to these top communities, we offer our product
for free and effectively. In exchange, we build up a great relationship
with the end user and we build up a great data set on who they are on a
deeper level. Use that to match people to jobs. Right now it\'s
effectively a marketplace with us, me specifically seeking out
employers, people that want to post to careerspan, leverage the network,
pay for access to all that talent effectively. All that good stuff.

**\
03:24**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay. Awesome. On my side of things, I work on user acquisition. So I
would be interested in getting users from your network to register for
Lensa job alerts. We have about 25 million jobs.

**\
03:41**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Wow.

**\
03:42**\
Mai Flynn\
That we advertise out. Yeah. We have organic jobs that we scrape from
Fortune 1000 companies. We have some direct agency employers, we have
ATSS and then just content from coming from other job boards as well.

**\
04:03**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Gotcha. To be to just so that I have the right understanding of the
situation. You\'re looking for folks to be on the lens of platform and
then I presume Lenza is like. I guess Lensa is compensated by the
employers that you post on the behalf of. Like. Do you all serve as a
like is the business model around distributing on behalf of companies to
talent? Is it. You know, is it just the posting wanted to sort of get a
sense of that as well. Mostly so that I can sort of properly frame the
way that career stats can add value.

**\
04:39**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah, for sure. We are just a job board aggregator. So we have clients
that pay us to distribute their jobs. And then on my side of things, I
am looking for new and returning job seekers who need that job content.
So if someone is looking to change careers or someone\'s just looking
for a different job, they come to us, look for different jobs and we
deliver email alerts, push notifications based on the job titles that
they\'re looking for.

**\
05:15**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Gotcha. And do you and what is the preferred way of y\'?

**\
05:21**\
Mai Flynn\
All.

**\
05:23**\
Vrijen Attawar\
So you know, again, obviously Lens is a huge website. I used it myself
when I was doing my MBA job hunting and quite like the experience and so
obviously you guys are operating at a certain scale. But the is. Is it
common practice for you to like forward jobs along to spread your reach?
Like working with someone like us to say, hey, career span, maybe you
can get this in front of eyeballs that we can\'t. Let\'s work with them
to get the job out. So long as there\'s traceability back to your folks
or kind of Delenza. Is that sort of the main thing that matters in terms
of justifying to the folks that pay you? Hey, we\'re bringing traffic
into you.

**\
06:05**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah. So we would pay you on either like a CPC or CPA basis. And
basically we are just looking for these users to register with Lensa so
we can monetize them based on the. The adverts that we have.

**\
06:22**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Gotcha.

**\
06:23**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah. And we. We purchase traffic through a few different channels.
Since we\'re in the job board industry, most of the partners already
work with each other. It\'s a little incestuous. So we do. We distribute
most of our jobs probably like 85, 90% of our jobs through XML feeds
through app cast traffic that way or we have a jobs API that you can
pull jobs from or we also do co registration so like a reg path where
you can have users opt into our job alerts and to sign up to. Let\'s we
can monetize that way as well.

**\
07:08**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Gotcha. Okay, that is all good to know. I mean naturally there\'s a, you
know, as tech companies there\'s a natural incentive to want folks to
actually be on your platform. And I say that to say that like we have a
jobs distribution sort of like rudimentary pipeline set up just to
describe that essentially we will go out and find folks that want to pay
career span to find a candidate as well as jobs that we scrape. Frankly,
all else aside, I would rather get jobs from y\' all because the place
that I have to scrape from, I then have to put in a buttload of effort
to pull a clean version of the JD. I\'m presuming based on how you\'re
describing your system that wouldn\'t be a problem with Lensa like the
way that you all are set up.

**\
07:55**\
Vrijen Attawar\
So already I\'m like very much in favor of taking your job\'s pipeline.
And then over and above that I would say that our engagement rate as a
reflection of our matching system, which I think someone in your
position can appreciate the challenges of matching people effectively
and having engagement through recommendations, we have somewhere between
75 to 80% open rate and about a 15 to 16% click through rate at any
given point in time because we so tightly match users to jobs. So I
think with us the only consideration may be that if we\'re evaluating
purely on a CPC basis, we may not look so hot just because we don\'t end
up sending a lot of volume. But we try to send folks that are highly
likely to enter into that pipeline or sort of be great candidates.

**\
08:51**\
Vrijen Attawar\
If that doesn\'t align with your incentives, we can obviously adjust the
thresholds a little bit to have a little bit more inflow. Happy to work
with you to make that happen. But by and large, the matching system and
the fact that we have such really great data on people makes us a lot
more equipped to match them than just off a resume.

**\
09:13**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah, for sure. I\'m checking out your website now. It\'s
mycareerspan.com correct? Yeah, this looks very interesting.

**\
09:25**\
Vrijen Attawar\
We are different. That\'s. I\'ll give us that. We are different.

**\
09:29**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah, I\'m hiring and I\'m job searching. Okay. So the jobs that you
have advertised under the job searching area, you would be able to pull
our jobs from our XML feeds or API to advertise onto there. And then we.
Based on the types of jobs that we have, they would be either rerouted
to Lensa to register or for the direct agencies employers, they would be
directed to apply. But you would have access to the 24 million jobs for
your candidates.

**\
10:10**\
Vrijen Attawar\
That would be incredible. Yeah. And we\'re happy to figure out like ways
to track. Well, you know, I think for us being able to even just pull a
pretty. Make a pretty standard sort of like set of API requests once a
week because for now we plan on focusing on product roles. I don\'t know
if there\'s a particular need for those kinds of candidates on your
platform. Hopefully there\'s always a need for product candidates, but
that\'s sort of what we\'re betting on is that the more subjective
nature of picking for product is going to play to our strengths. So
yeah, those are going to be the bulk of the candidates coming in, but we
do hope to expand to stuff like engineering and growth within the next
three months. So it\'s very much a temporary state of affairs.

**\
10:57**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay, that works for us. And we can also just give you specific job
categories since you\'re only starting with the product roles. So if you
want us to cut down our files just so you\'re not processing a few
million jobs at a time based on the job titles you\'d like, we can test
with those and then slowly expand. Expand out to the other industries as
you expand as well.

**\
11:23**\
Vrijen Attawar\
That would be great. The sort of slight fly in the ointment for us is
that the V1 of the matching system that we\'ve set up is pretty cost
inefficient and so we generally try to process. Again, this is very much
possible to be upgraded quite extensively, but I think at the moment
we\'re targeting about 80 to 70 to 80 jobs a week to distribute to about
700 or so folks. 700 to 1000. But these are pretty activated folks.
They\'re people that have done one or more career coaching stories. So
that\'s like 20 minutes minimum spent with career span, not to mention
the data you get from that. So yeah, happy to start handing those out.
Having a subset by product would be super valuable, specifically Boston
SF, NY roles. And then we try to post roles that have only been posted
every three days.

**\
12:16**\
Vrijen Attawar\
But at the same time, if we just set up a repeat, like an automatic
pipeline, we can just have stuff come over, clean it up, handle it on
our end, so we can try to set it up in a way that there\'s like very
little tinkering from Your end.

**\
12:29**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay. And then the signup process, it\'s a free signup, correct?

**\
12:34**\
Vrijen Attawar\
That\'s right. That\'s right.

**\
12:36**\
Mai Flynn\
All right. Just because we want to keep the user flow as relatively
friction free as possible since Lensa does already provide these jobs
for free if you go on to Lensa. So we\'re just trying to tap into your
audience just to see if there is a way for us to give your candidates
more options, what we can.

**\
13:02**\
Vrijen Attawar\
What the easiest way to start there then I think would be to distribute
jobs via email to Lens. The jobs as the application path itself. So in a
lot of cases the jobs are being sourced through us. We\'ll use a native
application path or a magic link. But in your case we\'ll route it to
Lensa and we\'ll be able to see pretty clearly what is the click
through, what are the stats around, like how many folks are actually
ending up on Lensa and the current sort of like size of user base. And
then we\'re hoping to as of next week onwards start to onboard about 200
to 400 folks a month or sorry a week onto Careerspan, we\'re hoping to
do a community a week effectively.

**\
13:49**\
Vrijen Attawar\
So the hope is very much to expand that to a much larger crowd and
expand it to maybe closer to like 200, 300 rules being distributed maybe
twice a week.

**\
14:01**\
Mai Flynn\
All right, that sounds like our team is willing to test that out. Just
because you guys are new. It\'s the industry that a lot of the users
just bounce from one platform to the next one. So it\'s nice to tap into
just fresh job seekers.

**\
14:23**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Right. The good thing with us, and what I will say I\'m very proud of
about us is that the quality of engagement that you\'ll get through
careerspan I can guarantee is going to be much better than anywhere else
because of the nature of the product. We specifically attract people who
are not just conscientious enough to like do self reflection based
career coaching, but are also extremely engaged. So as an example,
generally we\'ll see 3 to 5% of careers fan users return within 6 to 12
months. Just regardless, like regardless of what we do, we see a base
rate of about 2 to 3% of our users per month are just people that have
reactivated from a time, you know, a while back.

**\
15:08**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I think the good thing with us is also that we, because we give people a
reason to engage beyond just the job search. Like you do something cool
this month, you go to Careerspan, you log the story great, you\'re up to
Date, you know, and so we\'re trying to cultivate this sort of like
always job ready, always ready to be scouted sort of approach to
careers. And so I think that\'s one benefit that admittedly we\'re
small, but I think as we sort of grow, just the quality of Canada that
you\'ll get from us I suspect will be much higher.

**\
15:45**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay, awesome. Yeah. For us to set up a test with your traffic, we\'re
just going to need to get you guys started on appcast. Have you guys
ever used it before?

**\
16:00**\
Vrijen Attawar\
I don\'t think so, no. No.

**\
16:02**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay, so I would need to get you guys a master publisher. So you guys
would be our publishers that we would be sending the XML feeds to. So
we\'ll need that account set up for you. I\'ll send over that
information. And then in terms of testing, we start with a test of 15
cent CPCS and then we\'ll do a starting budget of \$2,500 per feed. And
then as we get your users in, we can see how they\'re interacting with
our job alerts, see if we\'re making an ROI on them. And then from there
we can scale up or down. You\'ll be assigned two analysts, one for our
direct content and then another one for our lead gen content. And then
we\'ll be able to give you feedback on what basically like where we see
opportunity to grow.

**\
17:03**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Sounds good. This is really exciting. Thank you so much, Mei.

**\
17:07**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah. And then in terms of content, are you looking for like the organic
content, syndicated content, direct agency content.

**\
17:21**\
Vrijen Attawar\
In terms of jobs? Right. I mean, I honestly like, short of picking the
pool that has the most ability to match, like where do we make the most
money, you know, because frankly it\'s. I think it\'s just like best to
put them in the most sort of high value channels for Lensa and for us
because I know that the candidates will be good for it. Yeah. That\'s
what we put in all this sort of effort for.

**\
17:53**\
Mai Flynn\
Okay, so we\'ll test out all of the content, see where we should put our
efforts, and then we\'ll grow the budgets that we can. We\'ll shut off
the tests that don\'t work out.

**\
18:04**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Sounds good. Sounds like a plan. Really excited to be working with such
pros. You guys have done this. Y\' all have been around for a while, so
you know we\'re going to learn a lot.

**\
18:13**\
Mai Flynn\
Yeah. So feel free to ask all the questions. Once I get our IO in, I\'ll
introduce you to the analysts and then we\'ll get a test started. For
October.

**\
18:26**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Sounds good to me.

**\
18:28**\
Mai Flynn\
All right, I\'ll send you a follow up email with the information I need
and then we\'ll go from there.

**\
18:34**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Sounds good. Catch you soon.

**\
18:36**\
Mai Flynn\
Thanks.

**\
18:36**\
Vrijen Attawar\
Bye.
